entry.title: Landed Cost in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Good day!

We have clients who have importations and would want to have the other landed costs capitalized as part of the cost of the inventory.  These same clients transitioned from Business One where Landed cost is a standard feature.  How is this being done in S/4 HANA public cloud?

 

Thank you and God bless.

 

-Melvin

entry.title: Re: how to export ldm from sap power designer to excel or...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can easily export almost all metadata for a single object type at a time into Excel. Simply go to the menu: Model → Entity Attributes.... Here, you can filter the specific properties of Entity Attributes you want to display (use the green ellipsis in the screenshot below). After that, you can easily export the entire list to Excel (red ellipsis in the screenshot below). You can repeat this process for any other object types (entities, relationships, inheritances, etc.).

This method works well for a one-time export. If you're looking for a more automated or sophisticated solution, you have the following options:

  1. Create List Report(s): These allow you to export (to CSV) directly from the Object Browser with just two clicks.
  2. Build a custom export function: This will perform the export exactly according to your needs and give you the flexibility to format the output Excel file(s) as desired. This is possible thanks to the extensive extension and automation capabilities of the tool.
  3. Create a custom JSON template: You can export by just two clicks after setting up your own JSON generation template.

Options 2 and 3 require some familiarity with PowerDesigner, but once you are comfortable, the possibilities are vast. If you need more help with this, feel free to reach out to me on LinkedIn - I`ll be happy to help.

Kind regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: 1 Item : Many Bin Locations per Warehouse in S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Good day!

We have clients where an item can have multiple bin locations in one warehouse.  Is this possible in S/4H Public Cloud and if not, what are the workarounds available?

Thank you.

Sincerely,

 

Melvin

entry.title: Re: Two-Tier ERP limitations
entry.content:
Hi,

Related to the question on "what if a sales subsidiary needs to run Advanced ATP to see stock real time of its manufacturing headquarter?" --> this is a valid scenario which is planned in future roadmap. If you would like to know / participate more on this, kindly visit the blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/the-cei-project-your-opportunity-to-influence-availability-check-in-two/ba-p/13734342

BR, Prakash Karkihallimath

entry.title: Java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out While connecting to outlook account
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to read email from an outlook account using mail adapter in SAP CPI/CI. While deploying it is giving error as: 

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][InstanceError] : {"message":"POLL_FAILED","parameters":["imaps://outlook.office365.com:993?binding=%23binding_MessageFlow_635&connectionTimeout=3000&delete=false&disconnect=true&fetchSize=20&folderName=Inbox&handleFailedMessage=true&mail.imap.auth.digest-md5.disable=true&mail.imap.auth.ntlm.disable=true&mail.imap.auth.xoauth2.disable=true&mail.imap.partialfetch=false&mail.imaps.partialfetch=false&maxMessagesPerPoll=20&mimeDecodeHeaders=true&peek=true&pollStrategy=%23mailPollStrategy_MessageFlow_635&scheduler=MessageFlow_635.pollConsumerScheduler&scheduler.cron=0+0%2F1+0-23+%3F+*+*+*&scheduler.timeZone=Etc%2FGMT&unseen=true"],"childMessageInstances":[{"message":"EXCEPTION","parameters":["javax.mail.MessagingException: * BYE JavaMail Exception: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out (local port 51856 to address 10.158.235.175 (551ab33c-9aa4-45b0-69c2-227d), remote port 993 to address 52.97.135.98)"],"childMessageInstances":[{"message":"CAUSE","parameters":["com.sun.mail.iap.ConnectionException: * BYE JavaMail Exception: java.net.SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out (local port 51856 to address 10.158.235.175 (551ab33c-9aa4-45b0-69c2-227d), remote port 993 to address 52.97.135.98)"]}]}]}

Please help to analyze this error.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Mayank

entry.title: Generate Booked Data based on attribute
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

i would need a hand for a data action that has the purpose of generating data, putting “0” on a “Dummy” measure based on an attribute of a dimension.
Let me explain, the user chooses via parameters the other combinations of dimensions.

Example:
User chooses as parameters:
Product= PRD1
Scenario= SCN1

 

EntityScoped
ENT1YES
ENT2YES
ENT3NO
ENT4NO
ENT5NO

The end result is as follows:

EntityProductScenarioDummy
ENT1PRD1SCN10
ENT2PRD1SCN10

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Validation in Rap Late Save Phase using CL_ABAP_BEHAV...
entry.content:
@abaper007 

Calling COBL_CODINGBLOCK_CHECK from RAP behavior method might not be a good approach. Can you check if the validate action of I_JournalEntryTP can be used in your scenario?

Regards

Lakshmi A

entry.title: FaceID for passcode for tablet (Android)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Can someone help me log in to C4C via passcode but with FACE ID? In the company, we use SAMSUNG tablets that are on Android OS, which only support the FACE ID unlocking method, we do not have fingerprints on them.

When logging in for the first time, when I get to the moment of entering the passcode, the application does not ask me to log in with FACE ID.

Is it even possible to log in with FACE ID on an Android tablet?

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Cloud APIs to create Customer Invoices?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have the requirement to upload Customer Invoices within S4Hana from another 3rd party system

is there an API of any kind to create a Customer Invoice within s4?
I am unable to locate one in the Business Hub, and the only one properly titled Customer Invoice is to read from S4 instead.

Could somebody help me identify such a service?

entry.title: Re: 1 Item : Many Bin Locations per Warehouse in S4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hi 

As per my knowledge it is possible in SAP S/4 hana public cloud. Best way you try to do proof of concept in your system

Here understand the bin location as storage bin location

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: 1 Item : Many Bin Locations per Warehouse in S4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hi Rashid,

Thank you for the reply.

In our scenario, the bin locations are all storage bins.  We have, let us say, Item A which can occupy multiple bin locations in one warehouse due to its sheer quantity. 

Can that be handled in the system?

Sincerely,

 

Melvin

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to propagate Table Column Comments to...
entry.content:
I'm looking to do this for views too.
Creating tables and views through the Catalogue/Repositories.
For a table we can do this:
{name = "COL_NAME"; sqlType = NVARCHAR; length = 128; comment="Columns comes from table X";}

Want to do similar for views when creating them like this:
schema="ETL";
public=false;
query="
select COL_NAME          // somehow add a comment to this view-column
from xxx
";

entry.title: can someone please help me solve this IPA_monitoring_alerts_test_sap_ipa_20025
entry.content:
I have configured the smtp mail in SAP BTP destinations. but when i try to test it using SAP BTP build process automation control tower->mail server i get this error  IPA_monitoring_alerts_test_sap_ipa_20025. and the mail is not sent. as i am still learning i don't have access to the support documents. can someone please help me. I want to know is there any specific components/service i need to enable in BTP for this to work or if i have missed out on something. 

please provide some solution for this. thank you.

entry.title: Work in Progress (WIP) account - G/L account type
entry.content:
Hello,

I am doing configuration for posting rule in WIP calculation tcode OKG8. In OKG8, we can defind P&L account and Bal. Sheet Account for WIP caluation. 

I know the WIP P&L account should be defined as Nonoperating Expense or Income G/L account type. So it will not have cost element. If it is create as cost element, we will get error message KQ 119. when we run settle the production order. 

But I want to understand what is the logic behind this design. Why WIP P&L account should not be a cost element.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Cancelpick EWM error message code is /SCDL/ICC001
entry.content: Thank you,

entry.title: L_REF_MULTIPLE_PROCESS_START (LT42) not creating Allocation TOs until all Picking TOs are completed
entry.content:
Hello,

The request from business side was to implement the two-step picking for TRs, and to automatically create a 2nd step TO as soon as the 1st step TO is confirmed.

We've managed to do that with implementation of FM L_REF_MULTIPLE_PROCESS_START (which behaves the same as LT42 transaction) in one of the user exits at TO confirmation, and it is functioning correctly as long as there is a single TR item in a TR. As soon as there are multiple TR items, the FM will not work until all the 1st step TOs for all TR items are completed, since it will throw an error message that "No quantities are available" or something similar.

This error message is in fact true for some of the TR items, but all other items are ready for 2nd step TOs.

Is there a possibility to target only TR items which are complete with this FM? 

Creating TRs with a single item is not an option, since they often manually create TRs with 30+ items and creating a separate one for each quant would be a nightmare. 

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Which is preferred : SAP Certified Enterprise Architect OR TOGAF Certified EA ?
entry.content: Exploring SAP alongside TOGAF is a solid approach. As for your question about whether an SAP Certified EA practitioner is preferred over TOGAF Certified EA in the SAP ecosystem, it really depends on the specific role and the company’s focus. Some places value the SAP certification more, while others appreciate the broader perspective TOGAF brings.When I was working on my certifications, I took an accredited TOGAF course, and it opened my eyes to how different frameworks can work together. It helped me see the innovative angles in traditional EA models, especially when integrating new ideas. The "Intelligent and Sustainable Enterprise" concept definitely brings fresh perspectives that can really enhance the overall approach.

entry.title: SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
When I open a BAS session, I can't see the Cloud Foundry icons in the left column.

I'm using a trial version.

Anything am I missing?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: How can I generate an XML with my EDOC_COCKPIT?
entry.content:
Hi @walkerist,
The eDocument Cockpit uses the SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF) to map the transactional data to the required XML format.
For more detailed information, please follow this guide : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/83f4631d77654e14800e31b17fe9bd45/6bf91193c7e84f2e9ae8732f7ac590ac.html

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content:
You should not use SAP standard tables in your data model as this is not clean core compatible. Instead, you should search for released CDS views.

I also created a small example in an SAP BTP 2408 system. When you start the preview of the service binding (OData V2 - UI), you cannot change employees but change the documents. The preview of the service binding corresponds to a vanilla Fiori elements LROP. See attachment.

entry.title: Show Spellcheck only if, Suggested Keyword have valid result
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a requirement in my project as follows.

Customer site is implemented with Spell Check. Whenever we initiate search, Search result come with Spell check/ Spell suggestions. 

However, when user click on spell check link, there are scenarios where User is getting empty results. This is a bad experience for user.

That is,

1. If user search "ADE", Then search result may come empty or there will be a search result.

2. Along with Step 1, Keyword suggestion will come "ACDE"

3. When user click on "ACDE", User will get empty result. This is a bad user experience.

 

Hence We are asked to do a change in a way that, the suggested spelling "ACDE" should show only if "ACDE" will give search results. 

In other words, Spell check keyword should not show if it does not have any search result.

Can anyone help me here?

 

Regards,

Eldhos Kurian

 

Regards,

Eldhos

entry.title: Re: System behaviour between EC and Compensation Compa-Ratio
entry.content:
Hi,

We had a similar issue where random mid point were picked for certain employees. We did some investigation and found that we had only selective countries in pay range combination and many small countries were not there for pay ranges

We then upload dummy  "0" value for the pay ranges for all such small countries where pay range actually did not exist and then it solved the problem for us. 

We ensured that each employee in the system has a unique match to the pay ranges and this sorted our problem. Completely FIXED it !

entry.title: Re: REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT F4 Help is not working
entry.content:
The REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT function module wasn't really designed for data entry.
Take a look at function modules such as POPUP_GET_VALUES or POPUP_GET_VALUES_USER_CHECKED.

entry.content:

For more information on Bring Your Own Rates refer to the help portal document.

entry.title: Re: Customer Standard Return - Returns Overview - Returns...
entry.content:
Hello @david_maksymec ，

Thanks for your question. To address your requirement of developing a report that captures the return steps, including the goods movement to scrap, and relates them to the return order, you need to understand the underlying data model and relationships in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Here I can provide approach to achieve this:

Step 1:The key entities involved in the return process are:
- Return Order
- Return Delivery
- Goods Receipt
- Inspection
- Logistical Follow-up
- Credit Memo Request
- Goods Movement (including movement to scrap)

Step 2: Identifying Relevant CDS Views and APIs:

1. **CDS Views:**
- **I_ReturnOrder**: Provides details about the return order.
- **I_ReturnDelivery**: Provides details about the return delivery.
- **I_MaterialDocument**: Provides details about material documents, including goods movements.
- **I_InspectionLot**: Provides details about inspection lots.
- **I_CreditMemoRequest**: Provides details about credit memo requests.
- **I_GoodsMovement**: Provides details about goods movements.

2. **APIs:**
- **API_RETURN_ORDER_SRV**: For return order details.
- **API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV**: For material document details.
- **API_INSPECTION_LOT_SRV**: For inspection lot details.
- **API_CREDIT_MEMO_REQUEST_SRV**: For credit memo request details.

Step 3: Establishing Relationships
To establish the relationship between the return order and the goods movement to scrap, you need to trace the document flow. Here’s how you can do it:

1. **Return Order to Return Delivery:**
- Use the `I_ReturnOrder` CDS View to get the return order details.
- Use the `I_ReturnDelivery` CDS View to get the return delivery details related to the return order.

2. **Return Delivery to Goods Receipt:**
- Use the `I_MaterialDocument` CDS View to get the goods receipt details related to the return delivery.

3. **Goods Receipt to Inspection:**
- Use the `I_InspectionLot` CDS View to get the inspection details related to the goods receipt.

4. **Inspection to Logistical Follow-up:**
- Use the `I_GoodsMovement` CDS View to get the logistical follow-up details related to the inspection.

5. **Logistical Follow-up to Goods Movement to Scrap:**
- Use the `I_GoodsMovement` CDS View to get the goods movement to scrap details.
- Ensure that the goods movement to scrap is linked to the logistical follow-up step.

Step 4: Creating the CDS View or Report
You can create a custom CDS View or report that joins these standard CDS Views to fetch the required data.

Conclusion
By following the above steps, you can create a report that captures the return steps, including the goods movement to scrap, and relates them to the return order. This approach leverages standard CDS Views and APIs provided by SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ensuring that the solution is robust and maintainable.

Hope it can help.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: The evolution of order management in Retail/CPG on the journey to unified commerce
entry.content:
In the world of unified commerce, customers and products are everywhere. Orders can come in from any channel — online, mobile, social, service centers, stores, marketplaces, and pop-up sources. And products can be located everywhere — in warehouses, stores, or with third-party vendors. As a brand, you are probably facing the challenge of bringing both dimensions together, with a seamless experience while maintaining profitability. But how do companies achieve this?

On the way to true unified commerce, companies usually mature through several evolutionary stages of order management.

Evolution Stage 1: Start with Existing Order Management Capabilities in ERP

In the beginning, brands often leverage the existing order management capabilities of their ERP systems. This approach is initially cost-effective and convenient, given that the ERP is already in place and familiar to the organization. For instance, a small retail company might use its ERP to handle inventory, order processing, and basic customer data management.

However, as the business grows and diversifies its sales channels, the limitations of the ERP become apparent. ERP systems are primarily designed for back-office operations and often lack the flexibility needed for modern, customer-centric order management. Customization projects to extend ERP capabilities quickly become expensive and complex, requiring specialized knowledge that may not be readily available.

Evolution Stage 2: Leverage Commerce Capabilities

Seeking greater flexibility, many brands turn to commerce solutions. These platforms offer more adaptable and user-friendly order management features, allowing businesses to handle a higher volume and variety of transactions. For example, a mid-sized fashion retailer might implement a commerce platform to better manage online and in-store sales.

Commerce solutions keep the tech stack simple and offer a range of tools for managing order processing, promotions and customer data. However, as brands scale and add more channels, they encounter the "omnichannel wall." Effective distributed order management requires a centralized data repository, a role that commerce platforms are not designed to fill. Consequently, businesses must either implement complex integrations or seek more comprehensive solutions.

Evolution Stage 3: Complexity Grows and Grows

As brands continue to expand their omnichannel capabilities, the complexity of their systems increases. Internal teams develop custom code and modules to connect various sales and service channels. For example, a large electronics retailer might have separate systems for online orders, in-store pickups, and third-party marketplace sales, all interconnected through point-to-point integrations.

This complexity leads to higher maintenance costs and a reliance on key in-house experts who understand the intricacies of the system. Innovation becomes harder to scale, and finding talent with the necessary expertise can be challenging. At this point, brands often face a critical decision: should they continue to build in-house or should they invest in a more robust, specialized system?

Evolution Stage 4: Adopt an Order Management System

In the final stage, brands adopt a dedicated Order Management System (OMS) that serves as a central intermediary layer between front-office and back-office operations. An OMS provides the necessary capabilities to manage orders across multiple channels efficiently and can integrate seamlessly with existing systems.

For example, a global sportswear brand might implement a cloud-based OMS that offers scalability, expert support, and the flexibility to innovate rapidly. The OMS becomes the backbone of the unified commerce strategy, enabling the brand to provide a consistent and efficient customer experience across all touchpoints.

With access to industry experts and solution integrators, brands can focus on innovation and growth instead of dealing with the complexity of maintaining a fragmented tech stack. A well-implemented OMS allows for real-time inventory visibility, streamlined order fulfillment, and improved customer satisfaction, driving profitability and long-term success.

Each evolutionary stage presents unique gains and challenges. In Stage 1, using existing ERP capabilities is cost-effective and familiar but becomes stiff and costly to customize as business grows. Stage 2 introduces commerce platforms that offer flexibility with a simple tech stacks, yet struggle with distributed order management, requiring complex integrations. Stage 3 brings custom code and omnichannel connectivity, enhancing tailored solutions but increasing system complexity and maintenance costs. Finally, Stage 4 adopts dedicated Order Management Systems (OMS), providing scalability and seamless integration, significantly improving operations and customer satisfaction, though it demands investment.

For brands ready to advance to Stage 4, SAP Order Management Foundation offers a powerful SaaS OMS designed to meet the demands of unified commerce. Together with further tools from the Retail Industry Cloud, it provides scalability and seamless integration ensuring efficient order management across all channels. By adopting SAP Order Management Foundation, brands can achieve real-time inventory visibility, streamline order fulfillment, and enhance customer satisfaction.

Interested in learning more about how SAP Order Management Foundation can transform your business? Reach out for more details and discover how we can help you achieve unified commerce success.

entry.title: Re: GL creation date in SAP ByD
entry.content: Thanks Frank, This is exactly what I was looking for:)

entry.title: Re: Can we fix the player bar location at bottom in SAP E...
entry.content: In the producer, go to Tools -> Settings -> Playback settings -> Trainer - Global -> Control Bar -> Orientation-> Bottom.

entry.title: Re: Brazil: pricing procedure
entry.content: When customer is in Brazil , if conditions can be print they will be used for determination of the price to print in smartform. SAP does not check if the condition is active or not

2) SAP HCM and SAP HCM PAYROLL

THANKS

entry.title: Re: cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download - separator firlds
entry.content:
Please read the official documentation of GUI_DOWNLOAD: WRITE_FIELD_SEPARATOR values can only be 'X' (not space) for adding a tab between the fields, or space for not adding one.

Why not just using GUI_DOWNLOAD the simple way, by formatting the lines directly?

e.g.

  DATA string_table TYPE TABLE OF string.
  DATA file_full_path TYPE string.

  string_table = VALUE #( ( `C|XPTO|NTRF|tablevalue|CF|USD` ) ).

  cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download(
    EXPORTING
      filename = file_full_path
      filetype = 'ASC'
    TABLES
      data_tab = string_table
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 17 ).

entry.title: Font size increase on SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have implemented Customer Checkout on a client. Client is complaining about the font size of UI, they wants to increase font size of Sales screen and reports. Is there any possibility to increase font size of Customer Checkout ?

entry.title: Re: Brazil: pricing procedure
entry.content: Only one basic price can be active per sales order item, after you have to manage discounts and increase

entry.title: Re: REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT F4 Help is not working
entry.content:
Why don't you create your own (popup) screen with classic ALV? It's very simple and you can do whatever you want. Also, note that REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT is not publicly released.

Anyway, no issue for me, F4 works well.

The screen of REUSE_ALV_POPUP_TO_SELECT is really old, obsolete and ugly! (ABAP List)

entry.title: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreement / Schedule Lines
entry.content:
Hi SAP Masters,

I am a recent SAP S4HANA Public Cloud User with EWM and found out that in SAP universe, when there is an Outbound Delivery created, this reduces our Open Quantity as if this was already shipped, but since it is only created and we are waiting for the truck to arrive, it is a bit misleading to say that the quantity is already delivered, could anyone share any suggestion on how to tackle this simple problem?

We would like to know all real open quantities, not 1 by 1 check.

To be able to check also our real backlog with our customers

entry.content: I sent an email a couple of days ago but if you signed up yesterday or the day before you might have not gotten it. I will trigger it again 🙂 But as we have 44 seats and 41 signed up, you are welcome to come on Friday!

entry.title: Re: SAP ByD Error: System automatically links wrong purch...
entry.content:
Hello M,

Thanks for your comment!

Unfortunately that doesn't solve the problem, since the comapny assignment of the employees is correct:
Colleagues from company B don't have any problem recording their times to the project over the PO.
The problem is, that the colleague who already belongs to company A (which owns the original project) can't record his times because the system always automatically pulls the PO for company B where none is needed.

entry.title: Re: Review Booklet Designer Add More Analytical Queries
entry.content:
Hello Henrike,

it is not possible to add your own queries to the existing ones.

king regards

Mert

entry.title: Re: Payroll Result after Payroll Run in SAP HCM
entry.content:
Hi Vikas,

You can use wage type reporter (PC00_MXX_CWTR) and Pay Reconciliation report (PC00_MXX_REC) to check detail pay summary for multiple associates.

Here XX is molga for which country payroll you want to see the pay summary.

Thanks

Gyan

entry.title: Re: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no...
entry.content:
Hi 

@Greg_Starke, I only just came across your question.

I understand your question. It seems that there are ever changing ways to connect to connect to 'SAP'. My answer is perhaps more a perspective and to perhaps raise discussion / debate. 

For CALM the 'managed system' set up (/SDF/ALM_SETUP) allows for a Proxy Host, Proxy Port, Proxy Username, Proxy Password. Much as my PC would use to access the internet.

Big picture it does seem the SAP approach is to move to standards and to centralise the network security.

In time sequence as far as I know:

  1. SAPROUTER - installed and configured by Basis (??). This would be in a DMZ but nevertheless be exposed to the internet and those supporting it would need to ensure it was hardened and monitor the security
  2. Cloud Connector. A reverse proxy. Once again installed and configured by Basis (??) and in the DMZ. More modern and only allows connections to be initiated from inside your network (that's how I understand it). Once again this would be in a DMZ but nevertheless be exposed to the internet and those supporting it would need to ensure it was hardened and monitor the security
  3. Connection to CALM using the organisations standard proxy (or perhaps  one dedicated to servers making connections out for Software updated, etc ?)

While I at 1st wasn't sure about the approach, after some thought it made more sense that the security / network team would worry about the connection to the internet and the SAP team would simply take advantage of it. 

Gavin

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Welcome

entry.title: Sandbox system for Flexible Workflow?
entry.content:
Hello!

So I am looking to test this Flexible Worklfow out, to try different scnearios, for example setting it up like a 'real-time' workflow scenario for PO. I would love to know if there's somthing practical (sandbox environment), anything about Flexible Workflow that I can play around with, or at least have some sort of activity, instead of only being able to 'read' about this and watch youtube about a standard procedure for Flexible Workflow. The documentation is great, but it's limited to practical understanding.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SD/EWM Real Open Quantities in Sales Schedule Agreement / Schedule Lines
Appraisal-Item categories in SAP SD
entry.content:
In SAP Sales and Distribution (SD), item categories play a crucial role in determining how sales documents are processed. Each item category controls various aspects of sales transactions, including pricing, billing, delivery, and inventory management. Here are some common item categories used in SAP SD:

1. **Standard Item (TAN)**:
- This is the most commonly used item category for selling products. It allows for pricing, delivery, and billing.

2. **Service Item (SERV)**:
- Used for services instead of goods. This category typically does not involve inventory management and is often tied to service contracts.

3. **Text Item (TXT)**:
- Used for non-billable items such as comments or notes. It does not affect pricing or inventory.

4. **Free of Charge Item (NORM)**:
- This item category is used for items provided at no charge, allowing for the processing of delivery and billing without affecting sales revenue.

5. **Subsequent Delivery (LUMF)**:
- Used for subsequent deliveries of previously sold items, often utilized in situations where partial deliveries occur.

6. **Consignment Item (CST1/CST2)**:
- These categories are used for consignment stock scenarios, where goods are stored at the customer’s location but remain owned by the supplier until sold.

7. **Returnable Packaging (VERP)**:
- Used for items that are returnable, such as crates or pallets. This item category helps manage inventory levels for packaging materials.

8. **Cash Sales Item (CS)**:
- Used in cash sales scenarios where the customer pays immediately, and the sales process is expedited.

9. **Intercompany Item (IV)**:
- Used for intercompany transactions between different entities within the same organization.

10. **Sales Order Item (OR)**:
- This category is used for processing orders that require specific handling or conditions.

Each item category has specific settings that influence the behavior of sales documents and the underlying logistics processes. The configuration of item categories can be customized to fit the specific needs of a business.

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:

entry.title: SAP Business One Client install on Azure Remote Desktop
entry.content:
Hi, we are testing at the moment so trying to find out the issues before we go live. We have migrated our SAP B1 v10 SQL server which was in house into Azure as a virtual Windows server. We plan to use Azure remote desktop for all the users to gain access. All users currently connect to a in house Windows Terminal server so hopefully the experience will be similar.

My question is regarding how to set up the Azure Remote Desktop. I have installed the SAP Client as well as the DI API app. My issue is we want to test it back to the live server, is it possible for the client which is on a non domain PC to connect to a domain server? There doesn't seem to be a way to make the authorisation across separate subnets and domains. Any advise / information on setting things up in Azure would be most welcome.

entry.title: Re: Font size increase on SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content: Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions. Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Good Afternoon all,
entry.content:
It sounds like you’re working on generating an 856 Advanced Ship Notice (ASN) and are running into issues with ensuring all necessary information is included. Here are some steps and considerations that might help you resolve this:

### 1. **Identify Data Sources**
- **Customer UOM (Unit of Measure)**: Check where this information is stored in your system. It might be linked to the product master data or customer-specific settings.
- **Part Number**: Ensure that the part number is correctly mapped in your system. This is often pulled from the inventory or product catalog.
- **Quantity Ordered**: Make sure this quantity is being captured during the order processing stage.

### 2. **Mapping Data for ASN**
- Ensure that you have a clear mapping of fields between your internal system and the EDI 856 document. The key elements usually include:
- **Product Identification**: Part Number, UPC, etc.
- **Quantity**: Ordered, shipped, etc.
- **UOM**: Ensure that this is in the correct format for the ASN.
- The structure of the 856 typically includes:
- **ISA Segment**: Interchange Control Header
- **GS Segment**: Functional Group Header
- **ST Segment**: Transaction Set Header
- **HL Segment**: Hierarchical Level (for shipments)
- **LIN Segment**: Item Identification (for part number and UOM)
- **QTY Segment**: Quantity Information

### 3. **Review EDI Implementation Guidelines**
- Refer to your trading partner’s EDI implementation guidelines for the 856 document. They might have specific requirements for the data fields that need to be included.
- Check for any required or optional segments that may be necessary for compliance.

### 4. **Extracting Data**
- Work with your development team or EDI software to ensure that data extraction from your system is set up correctly. If there are queries or data pulls that need to be adjusted, this would be the time to do it.

### 5. **Testing and Validation**
- Once you have made changes, test the output to ensure all the required information is included in the 856. Validate the document against your trading partner’s specifications.
- Consider using EDI validation tools to verify the structure and content of your ASN.

### 6. **Documentation**
- Keep documentation of your mappings and processes. This will help in troubleshooting any future issues and provide clarity for team members involved in EDI processes.

### 7. **Collaboration**
- If possible, collaborate with someone who has experience in EDI transactions within your organization. They might be able to provide insights based on their knowledge of the system.

entry.content:

We have implemented Customer Checkout on a client. Client is complaining about the font size of UI, they wants to increase font size of Sales screen and reports. Is there any possibility to increase font size of Customer Checkout ?

entry.title: Price changes in Service Order Item category SEW2:Fixed Price External Service Item.
entry.content:
Please tell me about price changes in Service Order Item category SEW2:Fixed Price External Service Item.
The process is as follows:

① Create the service order with the item category "SEW2:Fixed Price External Service Item". The price of that item is 1,000 yen.

② Release the Service Order and Create a Purchase Requisition.

③Create a Purchase Order based on the Purchase Requisition.

④After creating the Purchase Order, I want to change the price of the Service Order from ¥1,000 to ¥2,000.
　 However, I cannot change the price on the Service Order.

Please tell me how to change the price in this case.

 
Soma

entry.content: Hi @kammaje_cis , I would be interested in how you solved this problem. Would you mind sharing?

entry.title: Re: How to test sap btp api without passing the cookies?
entry.content: if you follow that blog correctly, make sure the url is *****abap*****, not ******abap-web*****, no "-web" in the url for the odata call.

We have the same requirement. We need to see the data in USD and BRL but Planning View only allows one currency. Does this needs a influence request?

Best Regards

Rohit

entry.title: How to Configure Automatic Rejection Emails 48 Hours After Disqualification on Business Days Only?
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting 

Hello,

I am currently working on configuring the automatic sending of rejection emails in our SAP system. The goal is to have these emails sent 48 hours after a disqualification occurs, but only during business days (Monday to Friday).

I would appreciate any guidance on how to set this up :

  1. Timing: Emails should be triggered 48 hours after a disqualification, that's easy to set up through  Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Settings > Return Email Address Information.
  2. Business Days Only: The emails should only be sent during business days, excluding weekends and public holidays > How to configure this ?

Could someone provide insights on the best way to achieve this in SAP? 

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Laura

entry.title: Re: Good Afternoon all,
entry.content:
Customer part number is E1EDL24-KDMAT.  No data exists in DESADV for customer UoM (which should match the order UoM), or quantity ordered.  There is information regarding the customer PO and PO line # which should be sufficient for a customer to find that information if necessary.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

Regards

Berlinda

entry.title: The value 'null' cannot be set as 'From' address in the email.

Log ID:3d64d18c-09af-462f-80f0-2614fc6-us10

entry.title: Enable Now users becoming inactive
entry.content:
I created around 250 users in Enable Now cloud edition (Version 10.8.5 build 4014) using the User Import functionality in the Active status. From the very next day, I have been noticing that every morning 180 of them become Inactive and cannot login. This has been happening for the last 2 days.

I am not able to pinpoint what happens overnight to switch them from Active to Inactive?

Please advise.

Parm

entry.title: Re: External Learning Request (not SF-182 related)

Happy to provide a demo if you reach out through https://store.sap.com/dcp/en/product/display-2001007210_live_v1/training-request-manager

entry.title: Re: Good Afternoon all,
entry.content:
Our customer unit of measure is stored in the material master as the sales unit of measure and it is different from our uom, so I guess what I am needing is the sales unit of measure in my IDOC as well as the standard unit of measure, the sales quantity uom can be 1 but because we have configured the item to a pack, the quantity actually shipped can be 10.

I need to be able to see all of this information on my idoc.

entry.title: Re: Default Values in Build Work Zone
entry.content:
I have created a feature request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/332744 . Feel all free to vote😊

Best regards, Matthias

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion content - Moving from test to production
entry.content: Anton and Shane are correct as always. Companion is designed to pull content from up to two workareas (Context Help and Guided Tours come from a designated workarea, and Learning App content comes from a designated workarea. Typically they are the same workarea but they do not have to be). You built your content in the designated workarea that Companion is configured to look at. By moving the content (unnecessarily, as Anton said), you probably now have it in a workarea that Companion is not configured to look at. SAP Enable Now does not work like SAP ERP systems, in that it does not require more than one workarea/instance. You build your content in the Production workarea of SEN, and you play/deploy it from there. There is no promotion from one workarea to another as content is developed.

entry.title: Get Column Index when click Cell on Data Table
entry.content:
Hi,

on OnPress Event of Data Table is there a way to get the index of column when I click a cell ?

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Custom Widget & Widget Add-Ons Samples Preview

Hi @marouferchichi ,

I followed the following steps:

1. downloaded json and zip file from github.

2. created the custom widget in SAC, using these files (without any modifications).

3. Included the funnel chart in my SAC story. I have used the same model and same KPIs used in the numeric pointers in the first box. However, the funnel chart doesnt show results.

Please let me know if I missed some key step.

Thanks in advance.

Madhu.

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP: Tenancy Models with SAP Cloud Services
entry.content:
Hi,

Is the model "Same Database, Separate Tenant DB" possible with SAP RISE Private Cloud Edition ?

It is explained in this blog... But we can see after that "The SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition is a single tenant landscape ..."

So can we, for example, use 2 tenants for 2 differents S4/HANA systems (in the same PCE contract)... In our case, for example, for 2 countries ? 

Thanks in advance.

Fabrice B.

entry.title: Can an individual have both an N ID and an NY ID at the same time?
entry.content: We have a case where some employees are moving from a mandated MR business to a non-mandated MR business.  For those totally migrated to the non-mandated business, they will have NY IDs.  For those that will be supporting both for a while it was recommended that they have both an N ID and an NY ID.  Is there a process defined on how to create the second ID?  SInce they currently have an N ID, we would be creating an NY ID to be used simultaneously with the N ID.

entry.title: Solution Process app dwonload best practices

entry.title: Re: Good Afternoon all,
entry.content: Let me clarify, the sales uom is PK order qty of 1 but our material attached to their part ordered is a uom ea but they are ordering 10 of our part.  We have created a material to represent their order requirements.

entry.title: Report/Dashboard tracking links between task,requirement,user stories
entry.content: When creating a task and requirement and/or user story in CALM, I couldn't find any report/dashboard in CALM or a download from CALM to excel to see the links between a task and requirement and/or user story.  When creating a task for example and a requirement and you would like to assign these task to the requirement it is not possible track those links in any report or dashboard. It is possible to download an excel sheet from the task app for "analysis" but there are no links between, or am I wrong?
